project_overview.project_name: KP- Spending Effectively for Enhanced Development (SPEED) Program
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve the availability and management of public resources for delivery of primary, middle and high school education and primary health care services.
project_overview.total_financing: US$400 million equivalent (SDR 278 million IDA Credit)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Primary, middle, and high school students and primary health care service users in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, with particular focus on girls and vulnerable populations

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - KP is designated as 'Fragile within a non-fragile Country' and includes Newly Merged Districts which have experienced conflict and require targeted support

key_components.description: Results-based financing supporting two result areas: (1) Providing adequate and reliable resources for delivery of education and health services through pension reforms, medium-term expenditure planning, staffing norms, and climate-resilient infrastructure; (2) Improved management of public finances through decentralization, IFMIS rollout, and e-procurement modernization
key_components.financing: US$704 million (US$385 million IDA, US$319 million Government)

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Strengthening capacity for transparent management of public finances
key_components.description: Technical assistance for MTFF development, e-systems upgrade, e-procurement implementation, cash management, and training of parent teacher councils and health management committees

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Improving PFM for delivery of education and health services
key_components.description: Technical assistance for costed MTEF development, facility-level budgeting e-solutions, e-payment mechanisms, gender budgeting framework, and PPP policy development

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Program implementation and accountability for performance
key_components.description: Technical assistance for service delivery standards, KPI development, citizen feedback systems, public participation in budgeting, monitoring units support, and gender analysis

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Finance Department, Government of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (GoKP), with coordination from Planning and Development Department, Department of Elementary and Secondary Education (DESE), Department of Health (DOH), KP Information Technology Board (KPITB), and KP Public Procurement Authority (KPPRA)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Program Steering Committee chaired by Additional Chief Secretary comprising secretaries of DOH, DESE, FD, Managing Directors of KPPRA and KPITB; Shared Services Unit (SSU) in FD for day-to-day management; Department-level working groups for sector-specific reforms; Independent Monitoring Units (IMU) in DESE and DOH for data collection
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: WB Procurement Regulations for IPF component; e-procurement system linked with IFMIS; requirement for approved procurement plans for budget releases; STEP system for monitoring
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Results Framework with PDO and intermediate indicators; Third-Party Verification Agent (TPVA) for DLI verification; Independent Monitoring Units in education and health departments; quarterly reporting to PSC; annual financial statements audited by Auditor General of Pakistan

identified_risks.risk_description: Political instability or changes in government priorities could affect sustained commitment to PFM reforms and education/health spending
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong government ownership demonstrated through complementary KP-RMRM program; Program Steering Committee oversight; legal covenants requiring implementation of Program Action Plan

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 pandemic impacts on economic growth (GDP contracted -1.5% in FY20), fiscal pressures, and potential reversal of poverty reduction gains; vulnerability to external shocks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Program supports fiscal space creation through pension reforms; MTFF development to improve budget credibility; federal transfers to provinces are critical assumption; Program designed to improve efficiency of existing spending

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak budget credibility, centralized financial control lacking automation at district level, low capacity in DOH and DESE, manual procurement processes, weak internal controls and accountability
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Shared Services Unit to provide FM and procurement support; IFMIS decentralization and e-procurement implementation; capacity building for M&E units; third-party verification of DLIs; annual audits by Auditor General; anti-corruption compliance reporting

identified_risks.risk_description: Low capacity for planning and financial management at facility level; weak accountability mechanisms; DESE implementing Bank project for first time; fragmentation of budget processes
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Technical assistance for capacity building; training of facility managers and parent teacher councils; establishment of facility-level budgeting systems; support for Independent Monitoring Units; phased implementation approach

identified_risks.risk_description: Complexity of vertical span from provincial to facility level; coordination challenges across multiple implementing entities; delays in budget releases; pension reform legal challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Clear institutional arrangements with PSC oversight; Shared Services Unit coordination; legal covenants; Program Action Plan with specific timelines; assumption that legal challenges to pension reforms are quickly resolved

identified_risks.risk_description: E-waste generation from IT equipment; environmental impacts from construction of boundary walls, toilets, water supply, electricity in schools/health facilities; social exclusion risks for persons with disabilities and vulnerable groups; inequitable access for indigenous peoples and NMD communities
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Systems Assessment completed; e-waste management guidelines to be developed; climate resilience framework for facility construction; gender-disaggregated monitoring; grievance redress mechanism; citizen engagement through budget consultations; targeted support for NMDs

identified_risks.risk_description: Lack of defined and costed sector plans; weak mechanisms for budgeting at district and facility levels; frequent supplementary budgets causing budget unpredictability
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: DLI 1 incentivizes development of costed MTEP; MTFF introduction; medium-term budgeting with integrated budget call circular; minimum threshold notification for non-wage recurrent financing

triggered_safeguard_policies: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts (Relevant)
Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management (Relevant)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Provincial level GRS to be established by March 31, 2022; World Bank's Grievance Redress Service (GRS) available; World Bank Inspection Panel accessible; existing program grievance redress mechanisms available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Program addresses gender gaps through: DLI 5 targeting filled female teacher positions in girls' schools; DLI 1 ensuring costed MTEP includes staffing for female teachers; PDO indicator tracking girls' schools with staffing norms; support for maternal and neonatal clinics; development of gender budgeting framework; monitoring of gender-disaggregated data on service delivery; targeted infrastructure (toilets, water, electricity) in girls' schools; female labor force participation rate in KP is only 11.3%, among lowest in Pakistan
vulnerable_groups: Girls and women (54% of school-aged girls in KP/NMDs are out-of-school vs 24% of boys)
Persons with disabilities (risk of marginalization if automation not disability-sensitive)
Newly Merged Districts populations (22% of out-of-school girls despite 15% population share)
Indigenous peoples and historically underserved communities
Persons with low IT literacy (risk of exclusion from digital systems)
Rural populations (two-thirds of schools lack drinking water, electricity; one-third lack toilets)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Program addresses conflict-affected context through: targeted support for Newly Merged Districts (NMDs) with specific DLI targets; climate resilience framework for disaster-prone areas (KP faces extreme precipitation and flash floods); support for service delivery in fragile areas; focus on improving basic infrastructure and service availability which are critical in post-conflict recovery; gender focus to address conflict-related disparities in education access
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: KP province designated as 'Fragile within a non-fragile Country'; Newly Merged Districts recently integrated after conflict; high vulnerability to climate shocks (floods, extreme precipitation); weak institutional capacity and governance; low human development outcomes; gender disparities exacerbated by conflict; pension expenditure consuming 20.6% of provincial fiscal revenues (up from 3.2% in FY09-10) constraining service delivery; social sector outcomes not matching increased investment
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Program supports improved service delivery and institutional capacity in security-sensitive province; focus on education and health service delivery can contribute to stability and social cohesion; climate resilience planning addresses disaster risks; no direct security operations but program operates in context requiring careful implementation oversight
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: No specific mention of refugee populations or displacement in PAD; however, program addresses service delivery needs in Newly Merged Districts which experienced conflict-related displacement; climate resilience planning addresses flood-related displacement risks; program supports basic infrastructure (water, sanitation, electricity) critical for vulnerable populations
